business.headline: Cognizant eyes up to 15,000 job cuts, India likely to see biggest impactThe possible job cuts come at a time when many large IT firms are restructuring. Slower spending by clients, rising automation, and the growing use of AI are changing the way companies operate.

Zee files case against Nykaa for alleged misuse of songs, seeks $210,000 damagesZee Entertainment has filed a lawsuit in the Delhi High Court, seeking damages of $210,000. The company claims that Nykaa used several of its copyrighted songs in Instagram reels to promote products to its large online audience.

Sensex, Nifty close lower as record-low rupee, oil worries weigh on marketsThe BSE Sensex fell 251.61 points, or 0.33%, to close at 77,017.79, while the Nifty 50 declined 86.50 points, or 0.36%, to settle at 24,032.80.
Gratuity rises under new labour rules, but what if you quit before 5 years?New labour laws may boost your gratuity, but does quitting before five years mean missing out completely? Let's have a look.

2% DA hike explained: Only Rs 600 more on a Rs 30,000 basic pay?A 2% Dearness Allowance (DA) hike may sound like good news, but the actual increase in your salary is fairly modest. On a Rs 30,000 basic pay, it adds just Rs 600 a month, so what's behind this small hike?
Biocon founder Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw names niece Claire Mazumdar as successorClaire Mazumdar is currently the founder and CEO of Bicara Therapeutics, a company that was incubated by Biocon.

Sensex, Nifty down: Why is stock market falling today?Markets had opened weak and extended losses through the morning, indicating that the election-led optimism seen in the previous session was short-lived.

Sensex, Nifty bounced back in April. What can investors expect in May?After a broad-based April rally, can markets sustain momentum in May as earnings take centre stage, separating fundamentally strong companies from momentum-driven stocks while global risks continue to cap sharp upside?

Sensex, Nifty slip at open as oil spike and weak rupee unsettle marketsThe BSE Sensex was down 170.40 points, or 0.22%, at 77,099.00 at 9:35 am, while the Nifty 50 fell 65.85 points, or 0.27%, to 24,053.45 as of 9:35 am.

Why Warren Buffett says he doesn't understand today's markets any moreAce investor and former CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, Warren Buffett, said today's market is harder to read, with trading increasingly driven by short-term bets.

Sensex, Nifty today: Will market fall amid weak rupee, elevated oil prices?Markets are headed for a cautious start as weak global cues, elevated oil prices and a soft rupee weigh on sentiment. Election results and quarterly earnings may still shape intraday moves across stocks.

OnEMI Technology IPO crosses 5x subscription on Day 3. Check latest GMPThe allotment of shares is expected to be finalised on May 6, 2026. The company is likely to list on the NSE and BSE on May 8, 2026.
NSE introduces electronic gold receipts to digitise gold tradingEach EGR is backed by real gold, ensuring that investors still own physical value, just without the need to store it themselves.

Govt shields fuel buyers, rules out relief for oil PSUs amid crude spike: ReportThe government has ruled out compensating state-run fuel retailers while keeping retail petrol, diesel and LPG prices steady. The move shifts part of the cost to bulk and industrial users as inflation concerns stay in focus.

FIFA World Cup 2026 faces broadcast uncertainty in India, ChinaIn India, a Reliance-Disney joint venture has offered $20 million for 2026 World Cup broadcast rights, a fraction of FIFA's ask, which was not acceptable to soccer's global governing body, two sources told Reuters on Monday.

Sensex, Nifty rise on election boost, gains capped by weak rupeeMarkets closed higher on Monday after early gains drew support from state election trends and earnings optimism. The rally lost steam later as a weak rupee, firmer crude and profit booking kept investors cautious.

Are rising fuel costs and global risks reshaping the aviation sector?With fuel costs climbing and geopolitical risks increasing, the aviation sector is feeling the strain, but is this a short-term phase or a lasting shift?

Your mobile number is key to your finances. Here's the upside and riskIn India, the mobile number is becoming the key identifier across banking, payments, loans and insurance. That convenience is widening access, but it is also sharpening concerns around fraud, privacy and control.

Explained: Why BHEL share price jumped 10% to hit upper circuit todayBHEL stock: BHEL shares hit the 10% upper circuit after the company reported a sharp March quarter earnings beat. The rally was driven by stronger profit, better margins, solid revenue growth and a dividend recommendation.

8th Pay Commission update: Where things stand after 6 monthsSix months since its formation, the 8th Pay Commission is showing signs of progress, but key recommendations, timelines and clarity on pay revisions for central government employees are still awaited.

entertainment.headline: Reunion alert? Kareena Kapoor in advanced talks for Salman Khan's superhero film Actor Kareena Kapoor Khan is in advanced talks to star opposite Salman Khan in Raj and DK's untitled superhero film. If the deal is sealed, it will mark Salman's first superhero project and their first on-screen reunion since Bajrangi Bhaijaan.

The Last Tenant starring Irrfan Khan, Vidya Balan releases after 25 years The Last Tenant, starring Irrfan Khan and Vidya Balan, was released on YouTube on his sixth death anniversary after being unreleased for nearly 25 years.

METkaKing: Was Vijay Varma at Met Gala 2026? Here's the twist Actor Vijay Varma shared images of himself as Brij Bhatti from Matka King at the Met Gala. The playful 'METkaKing' post tapped into the event's online buzz.

Shekhar Suman says talent beats visibility, cites Akshaye Khanna in Dhurandhar Shekhar Suman said that talent and strong performances matter more than social media visibility in today's film industry. He cited Akshaye Khanna's impactful role in Dhurandhar as an example of how a powerful performance can redefine an actor's relevance.

Suriya's Karuppu gets censored ahead of May 14 release. Here are the details Suriya's action drama Karuppu has cleared the censor process ahead of its May 14 worldwide release. The update keeps the film on schedule as interest builds around Suriya and Trisha's reunion and its divine justice theme.

Shekhar Suman on losing Devdas, finding Heeramandi and never chasing work Actor Shekhar Suman recalled turning down the role of Chunni Babu in Devdas in an exclusive interview. He said working with filmmaker Sanjay Leela Bhansali in Heeramandi later made up for the regret and introduced him to younger viewers.

Dug Dug review: Bizarre, funny, and unsettling ride through India's blind faithRelease Date:Fri May 08, 2026 Dug Dug movie review: Ritwik Pareek's Dug Dug follows a dead man's Luna as it turns him into Thakur Sa after repeatedly returning to the accident spot. The film uses the shrine's rise into a temple to trace how faith, spectacle and commerce merge in everyday India.

Vivek Agnihotri reacts to Bengal upset, recalls Mamata Banerjee's alleged 'assault' Vivek Agnihotri congratulated Bengal voters after the BJP ended Mamata Banerjee's 15-year rule and revisited his allegations over The Bengal Files. His note linked the election result to his "fight" against Banerjee.

Watch: Gaurav Khanna visits Siddhivinayak temple ahead of Khatron Ke Khiladi 15 Gaurav Khanna visited Mumbai's Siddhivinayak Temple with his parents before starting Khatron Ke Khiladi 15. His remarks to the media and the confirmed contestant list have added to the buzz around the new season.

Stood tall like a king: Shiva Rajkumar congratulates Vijay after mega win in polls Kannada actor Shiva Rajkumar posted a congratulatory message for Thalapathy Vijay on X, calling his party's win in the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections the actor's biggest victory.

Stood behind Kalaignar once, now destroyed his party: RGV reacts to Vijay's old pic Ram Gopal Varma shared an old photograph of M Karunanidhi and Vijay, and commented on the shifting landscape of Tamil Nadu politics. Vijay's TVK defeated Tamil Nadu giant DMK, headed by Kalaignar M Karunanidhi for 50 years.

Ssunita Ahuja on reuniting with Krushna, Kashmera, grandchildren: My heart's happy Sunita Ahuja reflected on her emotional reunion with her nephew Krushna Abhishek and his wife Kashmera Shah after 14 years. She shared that the moment was especially special as she met their children - Rayaan and Krishaang - for the first time.

When will this war end? Ameesha Patel's flight diverted as Dubai shuts airspace Actor Ameesha Patel said her flight from New York to Mumbai was diverted to Muscat, as Dubai closed its airspace due to fresh missile attacks.

Viral: Shah Rukh Khan, Deepika Padukone film romantic beach scene for King New visuals from King show actors Shah Rukh Khan and Deepika Padukone filming a romantic beach sequence in South Africa. The leak has kept attention on the film, which is slated to release on December 24.

The Odyssey new trailer: Matt Damon, Tom Holland, Robert Pattinson set up epic vibes Universal Pictures has unveiled a new trailer for Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey, showing Odysseus facing the Cyclops and Antinous eyeing Ithaca's throne. The footage sets up Nolan's next big-screen spectacle as the film heads towards a July 17 release and an IMAX first.

What a gift: Radikaa, Charmme wish Trisha on birthday, link it to Vijay's TVK win Actors Radikaa Sarathkumar and Charmme Kaur wished Trisha on her 43rd birthday and tied it to Vijay's TVK win. The posts drew attention as TVK secured 108 seats and online speculation around Vijay and Trisha continued.
Amitabh Bachchan mourns death of a fan, calls her family in emotional note Amitabh Bachchan wrote an emotional note mourning the death of a fan he considered family, calling her an "EF" on his blog.

Alia Bhatt, Aishwarya to Ammy Virk: Celebrities likely to attend Cannes 2026 Actors Alia Bhatt and Aishwarya Rai Bachchan are expected to return to the Cannes Film Festival this year. Meanwhile, actors Tara Sutaria and Ammy Virk are likely to make their Cannes red carpet debut.

Billie Eilish on James Cameron co-directing her concert film: Pretty magical James Cameron invited Billie Eilish to co-direct a 3D concert film based on her Hit Me Hard and Soft Tour. Eilish called the offer pretty magical as the project brings her live world into cinemas with new visual technology.

general news.headline: No question of my resignation, haven't lost election: Mamata Banerjee defiantMamata Banerjee rejects calls to step down after defeat, alleges large-scale voter manipulation and violence, and signals a renewed political fight even as INDIA bloc leaders rally behind her.

Kicked me in belly, threw me out of counting centre: Mamata on CRPF 'goons'A defiant Mamata Banerjee, addressing a press conference, claimed the TMC didn't lose the elections as 100 seats were 'forcibly taken away' and thus the question of her resignation doesn't arise.

WHO flags concern over suspected human hantavirus spread after cruise deathsHantavirus has long been seen as a rodent-borne threat. But emerging evidence that some strains may pass between humans is prompting fresh concern among health experts.

TVK plans talks with Congress, Left parties to form coalition govt: SourcesVijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), which won 108 seats in a strong debut in Tamil Nadu, but fell short of a majority, is eyeing talks with the Congress, Left and smaller parties to form a coalition government, sources told India Today TV.

Annamalai: The missing star of Tamil Nadu electionOnce projected as the BJP's face of expansion in Tamil Nadu, IPS officer-turned-politician K Annamalai found himself off the ballot as the party pivoted back to alliance with the AIADMK. What does his absence from the centre stage highlight? How does his absence reflect the BJP's own dilemma in Tamil Nadu?

UP ends prepaid smart meters, shifts all power connections back to postpaidUttar Pradesh government has scrapped mandatory prepaid smart meters and reverted all power connections to postpaid billing following consumer complaints.

Has Congress become the new Muslim League?The Congress won 21 of the 390 seats it contested in Assam and West Bengal. Of the 21 winners, 20 are Muslims. The party, which attacks the BJP as communal, is getting caught in its own trap. And the warning came from within Congress way back in 2014.

If Bengal wanted BJP, then Bengal has got BJP: Mahua MoitraMahua Moitra responded to the BJP's sweep in Bengal, saying her party fought against unimaginable odds on an uneven pitch. She also acknowledged the will of the people, framing her reaction around both the contest and the verdict.

Why Iran is striking UAE amid ceasefireThe UAE's status as a regional economic powerhouse, coupled with its close strategic ties to the United States and Israel, has made it a prime target of Iran, leaving the Gulf's most globally connected hub among the hardest hit by Iranian strikes.

3 parties, 3 seats: Lottery King Santiago Martin's family wins big in TN, PuducherryAadhav Arjuna, Leema Rose Martin, and Jose Charles Martin, the son-in-law, wife, and son of Santiago Martin, respectively, have won the election in Tamil Nadu and Puducherry.

Godrej Industries lays out 2031 growth ambitionsThe purpose statement, 'Crafting tomorrow since 1897', celebrates the Godrej Group's legacy while clearly positioning the new, refreshed Godrej Industries for the future.

How BJP has helped street fighter MamataMamata Banerjee faces a defining moment after the BJP swept both her Bengal bastion and Bhabanipur home turf. It has raised speculation on what might be Mamata's next political move.

From house help to MLA: BJP's Kalita Majhi wins seat as saffron wave sweeps BengalKalita Majhi, a domestic worker from Guskara, won the Ausgram seat as the BJP swept West Bengal. Her victory underscored the party's push for grassroots candidates amid a wider political shift in the state.

8th Pay Commission update: Where things stand after 6 monthsSix months since its formation, the 8th Pay Commission is showing signs of progress, but key recommendations, timelines and clarity on pay revisions for central government employees are still awaited.

India deploys silent warships to prowl the seas. Why this is a gamechangerA significant fleet expansion of the Indian Navy is picking up pace. The recent deliveries of INS Mahendragiri, INS Dunagiri and the commissioning of the INS Taragiri are testament to the Navy's shift towards multirole readiness, blue water operations, and sustained presence across the Indian Ocean. Here's what the Navy's Project 17A means for India.

Anthropic, OpenAI launch AI services companies, challenge TCS and Infosys in IndiaAnthropic has announced a new $1.5 billion joint venture with major Wall Street firms including Blackstone and Goldman Sachs. According to the AI startup, the new joint venture will be an AI services company that will sell its tools to companies.

Asansol Durga temple, shut for several years, reopens after BJP's huge Bengal winA Durga temple in West Bengal's Asansol reopened to devotees after remaining shut for several years due to local tensions. The move followed the BJP's sweep in Paschim Bardhaman district and in the Assembly polls.

High Court to appoint senior lawyers to represent Arvind Kejriwal after boycottHearing the CBI's plea challenging the discharge of Kejriwal and other accused in the liquor policy case, the bench indicated that it would make sure that they were represented by amicus curiae.

From college dropout to CM: How Vijay rewrote the scriptTVK Chief-turned Tamil Nadu CM Vijay's education journey, from a college dropout to a political leader, is gaining attention as his party performs strongly in Tamil Nadu. Alongside his rise, his promise of free higher education for children of small farmers has become a key talking point in his political campaign.
Biocon founder Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw names niece Claire Mazumdar as successorClaire Mazumdar is currently the founder and CEO of Bicara Therapeutics, a company that was incubated by Biocon.

Goons entered, beat me up: Mamata's explosive phone call on Bhabanipur lossTMC MP Kalyan Banerjee shared a phone call in which Mamata Banerjee alleged irregularities during Bhabanipur counting, claiming intimidation at counting centres and removal of her agents. She further accused procedural lapses in handling EVMs and said the outcome would be legally challenged with supporting evidence.

Noida university student slapped, verbally harassed by classmates in ragging caseA video showed an alleged case of ragging where a group of female students assaulted another student at a private university in Greater Noida. The clip has prompted an internal enquiry and renewed concerns over campus safety and anti-ragging measures.

Trapped with a virus: How an Antarctic cruise turned into horror at seaThe WHO said all affected passengers fell ill between April 6 and 28, with symptoms that rapidly progressed to pneumonia and acute respiratory distress.

Crocodile tracked by drone, cut open; man's remains, 6 pairs of shoes found insideRescuers in South Africa airlifted a giant crocodile after Gabriel Batista disappeared in floodwaters. The dissection found body parts of a man, widening the investigation.

Reunion alert? Kareena Kapoor in advanced talks for Salman Khan's superhero filmActor Kareena Kapoor Khan is in advanced talks to star opposite Salman Khan in Raj and DK's untitled superhero film. If the deal is sealed, it will mark Salman's first superhero project and their first on-screen reunion since Bajrangi Bhaijaan.

METkaKing: Was Vijay Varma at Met Gala 2026? Here's the twistActor Vijay Varma shared images of himself as Brij Bhatti from Matka King at the Met Gala. The playful 'METkaKing' post tapped into the event's online buzz.

sports.headline: MI need to look after Bumrah: Ravi Shastri decodes pacer's poor IPL 2026 form Former India coach Ravi Shastri blamed mental fatigue for Jasprit Bumrah's poor IPL 2026 form, saying the Mumbai Indians must carefully manage the workload of their pace spearhead.

Relief from Indian captaincy lifted Rohit Sharma's batting: Gavaskar after MI beat LSG IPL 2026: Sunil Gavaskar said Rohit Sharma's batting has grown since the captaincy burden was lifted. The remark came after Rohit's 84 off 44 led Mumbai Indians past Lucknow Super Giants.

I was never rated as a T20 player: Rahul glad to silence IPL strike-rate chatter KL Rahul admitted that things have not always gone his way in T20 cricket, but said he is proud to have broken the perception of being only a Test specialist after years of criticism over his strike rate and white-ball game.

No one really cares: Sat-Chi's viral cryptic post after Thomas Cup medal win Satwik Rankirajreddy and Chirag Shetty shared a cryptic Instagram story after India's Thomas Cup bronze. The post drew attention to the muted reception around the team's campaign and return home.

Gold medal in hand, no way home: Pole vault prodigy Kavinraja left stranded Pole vaulter Kavinraja S was stranded at Rajahmundry railway station for over 12 hours while returning from Bhubaneswar with his poles. The episode highlighted the travel difficulties athletes face while carrying specialised equipment.

Ollie Robinson sets sights on England Test recall after positive McCullum feedback England pacer Ollie Robinson said encouraging messages from head coach Brendon McCullum and managing director Rob Key have revived his hopes of returning to England's Test side.

Aimless Rishabh Pant had no clear-cut plans for LSG during MI loss: Shaun Pollock Shaun Pollock questioned Rishabh Pant's handling of Lucknow Super Giants' bowlers after the loss to Mumbai Indians. The criticism sharpened focus on LSG's fading playoff hopes and Pant's difficult season.

How MS Dhoni's World Cup-winning six inspired Raghu Sharma's IPL journey Mumbai Indians leg-spinner Raghu Sharma said watching MS Dhoni hit the World Cup-winning six against Sri Lanka at the Wankhede Stadium in 2011 inspired him to take cricket seriously despite weighing 102 kilograms and facing doubts over his future in the sport.

Who will telecast FIFA World Cup in India after Reliance-Disney deal collapse? FIFA has not finalised a broadcaster for the 2026 World Cup in India after rejecting the Reliance-Disney joint venture's bid. The standoff has left one of football's biggest markets awaiting a late rights deal weeks before kick-off.

DC batting overly reliant on KL Rahul: Ex-India star ahead of CSK clash IPL 2026: Aakash Chopra has said Delhi Capitals are relying too heavily on KL Rahul ahead of their match against Chennai Super Kings. The concern has sharpened as Delhi's brittle batting returns and painful home defeats leave little margin for error.

You should go home: How Ruturaj sparked Overton-Tilak clash during MI vs CSK Jamie Overton said Ruturaj Gaikwad's teasing on the previous ball fed the brief on-field exchange with Tilak Varma during MI vs CSK. Ruturaj revealed what he told to Overton, which led to the spat taking place.

What's wrong with Bumrah? Sunil Gavaskar explains MI pacer's IPL 2026 issuesSunil Gavaskar has said Jasprit Bumrah is overcomplicating his bowling during a lean IPL 2026 for Mumbai Indians. With Sanjay Bangar suggesting a short break, MI must decide how to restore his rhythm.

Foul play in RR sale? Kal Somani-led group say they were pushed out of dealThe Kal Somani-led consortium has claimed it was pushed out of the Rajasthan Royals ownership deal despite leading the bid for six months. The statement challenges reports that it withdrew and raises questions over how the franchise sale was handled.

Release Rishabh Pant as captain, we're losing him as batter: Pundits tell LSGRohan Gavaskar and Manoj Tiwary have urged Lucknow Super Giants to remove Rishabh Pant as captain after the defeat to Mumbai Indians. They said the burden of leadership is affecting his batting and LSG should let him play only as a batter.

When will Hardik Pandya return from injury for MI in IPL 2026?IPL 2026: Hardik Pandya missed Mumbai Indians' match against Lucknow Super Giants with a back spasm. His uncertain return leaves MI monitoring their captain closely during a crucial stage of IPL 2026.

Neymar accused of assault on Santos teammate after heated training clashIPL 2026: Santos are investigating an alleged training-ground altercation between Neymar and Robinho Jr. The episode has raised player-safety concerns and put the youngster's future at the club in focus.

Rishabh Pant's Rs 27 crore price tag under scrutiny? Langer defends LSG captainIPL 2026: Justin Langer defended Rishabh Pant's form and tactical calls after Lucknow Super Giants lost to Mumbai Indians. The support came as Pant's modest returns and LSG's inability to sustain strong positions stayed under focus.

No T20 talk or life-changing advice: How Rohit formulated Rickelton's MI riseRyan Rickleton said his discussions with Rohit Sharma are focused on batting, not only T20 cricket. He said Rohit's calm, clarity, and chemistry at the top are helping the Mumbai Indians stay alive in the playoff race.

Buoyed by Starc's return, DC aim to crush CSK as playoffs race intensifiesIPL 2026: Mid-table dwellers Delhi Capitals and Chennai Super Kings face off at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in Delhi. It is expected that Delhi will unleash their fierce pace attack on CSK, one that is bolstered by the return of Lungi Ngidi.

Premier League title race out of City's hands after Everton draw: GuardiolaPep Guardiola said Manchester City's 3-3 draw with Everton left the title race out of their hands as he conceded that the advantage is now with Arsenal.

IPL Play Of The Day: Rohit-Rickelton turn into MI's Batman-Robin to down LSGIPL 2026: Rohit Sharma made his Batman-esque return with 84, adding an opening stand of 143 with Ryan Rickelton as Mumbai Indians chased 229 against Lucknow Super Giants. The win lifted MI to six points and kept their slim playoff chances alive.

Paisa vasool: How Rohit Sharma vowed fans with stunning comeback vs LSGIPL 2026, MI vs LSG: Harbhajan Singh said fans got full value for money as Rohit Sharma's 84 off 44 powered Mumbai Indians to a thrilling chase of 229 against Lucknow Super Giants at the Wankhede Stadium.

Helpless Rishabh Pant left praying for luck as LSG sink deeper after MI defeatIPL 2026, MI vs LSG: Rishabh Pant cut a helpless figure after the Lucknow Super Giants suffered their sixth straight defeat, going down to the Mumbai Indians by six wickets on Monday at the Wankhede Stadium.

Rohit Sharma roars back to keep Mumbai Indians alive in playoffs raceIPL 2026, MI vs LSG: Rohit Sharma scored 84 off 44 balls as Suryakumar Yadav's Mumbai Indians beat Rishabh Pant's Lucknow Super Giants by six wickets on Monday, keeping their playoff hopes alive.

FIFA rejects Reliance-Disney $20M bid for 2026 World Cup India rightsFIFA has rejected Reliance-Disney's $20 million bid for India's 2026 World Cup broadcast rights package. With time shrinking, millions of fans could miss the tournament unless broadcasters strike terms quickly.

MI's Raghu Sharma celebrates maiden IPL wicket with Jai Shri Ram note: WatchIPL 2026, MI vs LSG: Raghu Sharma marked his maiden IPL wicket with an emotional note after dismissing Akshat Raghuwanshi. The leg-spinner also impressed with figures of 4-0-36-1, underlining his steady rise from domestic grind to the Mumbai Indians setup.

IPL: Delhi prepare to unleash hell on CSK as Lungi Ngidi returnsLungi Ngidi is available for Delhi Capitals' home match against Chennai Super Kings after completing the mandatory break. His return could allow Delhi to field him with Mitchell Starc in a key contest between two sides level on points.

'BJP agent Messi': Fans have field-day after party sweeps Bengal electionsThe BJP swept the Bengal elections marking the end of the 15-year-rule of the TMC. Amidst the frenzy, the sports fans wondered, was this loss triggered by Lionel Messi's visit to Kolkata?

Nicholas Pooran shakes off KKR horror, decimates Mumbai Indians with 16-ball fiftyIPL 2026, MI vs LSG: Nicholas Pooran silenced critics with a blistering 16-ball fifty, smashing 63 off 21 balls to power LSG after a poor run of form. He tore into MI's bowlers with fearless hitting before Corbin Bosch ended his stunning knock.

Who is Akshat Raghuwanshi? MP batter makes IPL debut against idol Rohit SharmaIPL 2026: Rishabh Pant confirmed Akshat Raghuwanshi's maiden IPL cap for Lucknow Super Giants against Mumbai Indians at Wankhede. The debut places a sought-after Madhya Pradesh batter on a bigger stage after strong domestic returns.

Vaishnavi Sharma stays grounded after T20 World Cup snub: Still a lot to learnVaishnavi Sharma has taken her T20 World Cup snub in stride, saying she has plenty to learn and a long career ahead. The youngster remains focused on self-improvement and growth, drawing confidence from her domestic success and early international exposure.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
